title: Cissus leucophlea
taxonomy: Cissus leucophlea is a species of plant in the family Vitaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as grape or wine vines. This family is part of the larger order Rosales, which also includes other fruit-bearing and flowering plants. The order Rosales is a group of flowering plants that are often characterized by having aggregate fruits and being woody or herbaceous in growth habit. Within the family Vitaceae, Cissus leucophlea is classified as a member of the tribe Viteae, a group of plants that are known for their woody vines and climbing habits. Cissus leucophlea is further classified as the only species in the genus Cissus leucophlea.
uses: Cissus leucophlebia, also known as the white grape or white ivy, is used in traditional medicine. Its leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve bone density, and aid in weight loss.
